findings.description: display_name, action, and created_at values from the database are interpolated into HTML table rows without html.escape, enabling stored XSS in the admin report via user-controlled display names or actions.

fetch_events docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the predicate created_at < end.isoformat() excludes every event on the end day for all plausible created_at formats (date-only, space- or T-separated timestamps).
title is substituted into the <title> and <h1> elements without HTML escaping, an XSS vector whenever the report title is attacker-influenced.
top_users uses the same end-exclusive created_at < end predicate, so end-day activity is silently omitted from rankings, inconsistent with the inclusive-range contract stated in fetch_events.
event_count (both branches, lines 82 and 88) excludes the end day via created_at < end, undercounting events relative to the module's documented inclusive boundary semantics.
fetchall with no LIMIT or pagination materializes the entire date range in memory and render_activity_report then builds the whole HTML as one string, so a wide range can exhaust memory.
The generated HTML declares no <meta charset> while save_report writes UTF-8, so non-ASCII display names can render as mojibake in browsers that assume a different default encoding.
A negative limit is passed straight through to SQLite where LIMIT -1 means unlimited, so an invalid limit silently returns all users instead of failing.
save_report writes to a caller-supplied out_path with no validation, allowing path traversal or arbitrary file overwrite if the path is ever attacker-influenced.
save_report writes non-atomically (no temp-file-and-rename), so a failure mid-write leaves a truncated report at out_path.
sqlite3.connect uses the default timeout with a fresh connection per call, so concurrent writers can surface 'database is locked' errors during report generation.
No validation that start <= end; swapped arguments silently produce an empty report rather than raising an error.
The unsanitized title flows into the log record, permitting log forging via embedded newlines if titles are user-influenced.
Row HTML is accumulated with += in a loop; ''.join over a list avoids the quadratic worst case, though CPython's in-place concat optimization makes this mostly a nit.
event_count duplicates the COUNT query across two branches that differ only by one predicate and could be unified by conditionally appending the action filter.
border="1" is a deprecated presentational HTML attribute; styling belongs in CSS.
